sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position Size = Account Size / (Max Loss per Trade / Optimal f). The optimal f value is variable and must be calculated from historical win rate and payoff ratio. Example: 50,000 / (5,000 / f) where f is the optimal fraction expressed as decimal.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing total account capital by the ratio of maximum loss per unit to optimal f fraction
